Sent by Theo at 16:22 - We can now confirm that all power in the Luton LU2 7 area should be back on. Our engineers have diverted power around an underground network fault to get the majority of customers back on. However, due to the location and complexity of the issue, we have also used a temporary generator to restore power to the last of the affected properties. This will provide power until our engineers plan in and safely complete the full repair, which may take a few days.

We're really sorry for any disruption this unexpected power cut caused and how long you were off for. If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Steve at 14:36 - Our engineers are continuing to work in the Birchen Grove area to restore your power as quickly as we safely can. We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. For more information, please see your terms of connection, which are usually provided by your supplier but are available here https://UKPN.short.gy/ToC. Thank you.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our engineers have just called in to advise us the temporary generator has arrived, however it is missing some equipment needed. This is being resourced now but unfortunately, it's going to take us past our current time frame to complete this safely, so we're now working towards between 15:30 and 16:30 to have power back on.

We can appreciate how frustrating and disruptive being without power can be, and our staff are working as fast as they can. If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to speak to our team, but I'll let you know further news as it comes in from site. Sent by Will at 13:33.
